mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Persistent high unemployment (UNRATE > 6.7%) signals weak labor market conditions that depress corporate earnings growth, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite high unemployment, or UNRATE fails to correlate with returns (Pearson r < 0.1).

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in inflation (CPIAUCSL > 265) and falling interest rates (FEDFUNDS < 1%) create stagflationary pressures that reduce equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX returns remain positive in 70%+ of cases where both CPIAUCSL > 265 and FEDFUNDS < 1%, or the interaction term loses predictive power (p > 0.1).

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production (INDPRO < 103.5) indicates weakening manufacturing activity that reduces corporate profitability, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60d returns in > 50% of triggered instances, or INDPRO fails to correlate with returns (Pearson r < 0.15).

mechanism_narrative: Rising housing starts (HOUST > 1500) signal improved consumer confidence and construction activity that boost corporate revenues, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX returns remain negative in 70%+ of cases where HOUST > 1500, or the series loses predictive power (p > 0.1).

mechanism_narrative: Strong consumer spending (RSAFS > 550,000) indicates robust demand conditions that drive corporate earnings growth, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20d returns in > 50% of triggered instances, or RSAFS fails to correlate with returns (Pearson r < 0.2).

mechanism_narrative: Conflicting signals between rising payrolls (PAYEMS > 140,000) and high unemployment (UNRATE > 6.5%) indicate labor market distortions that create uncertainty, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX returns remain positive in 70%+ of cases with conflicting PAYEMS/UNRATE signals, or the interaction term loses predictive power (p > 0.1).

mechanism_narrative: Monetary tightening cycles (FEDFUNDS > 4%) increase borrowing costs and reduce corporate investment, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 50% of triggered instances, or FEDFUNDS fails to correlate with returns (Pearson r < 0.15).
